{{< include latex-macros/macros.qmd >}}

:::{#exr-logistic-loglik}

Find the log-likelihood function for the general logistic regression model.

:::

{{< slidebreak >}}

:::{#sol-logistic-loglik}

$$
\ba
\ell(\vb, \vy)
   &\eqdef \logf{\Lik(\vb, \vec y) }
&& \text{(definition of log-likelihood)}
\\ &= \logf{\prodin \Lik_i(\vb, y_i)}
&& \text{(likelihood factors, by independence)}
\\ &= \sumin \logf{\Lik_i(\vb, y_i)}
&& \text{(log of a product is the sum of the logs)}
\\ &= \sumin \red{\ell_i}(\pi(\vx_i))
&& \text{(definition: } \red{\ell_i} \eqdef \logf{\Lik_i} \text{)}
\ea
$$ {#eq-loglik-bernoulli-iid}

Using @thm-logodds-pi and @cor-inverse-odds-nonevent:

$$
\ba
\red{\ell_i}(\pi_i)
   &= y_i \logf{\pi_i} + (1 - y_i) \logf{1-\pi_i}
&& \text{(log of the Bernoulli likelihood } \pi_i^{y_i}(1-\pi_i)^{1-y_i} \text{)}
\\ &= y_i \logf{\pi_i} + 1 \cd \logf{1-\pi_i} - y_i \cd \logf{1-\pi_i}
&& \text{(distribute } (1-y_i) \text{)}
\\ &= y_i \logf{\pi_i} + \logf{1-\pi_i} - y_i \logf{1-\pi_i}
&& \text{(multiplicative identity: } 1 \cd \logf{1-\pi_i} = \logf{1-\pi_i} \text{)}
\\ &= y_i \logf{\pi_i} - y_i \logf{\teal{1-\pi_i}} + \logf{1-\pi_i}
&& \text{(commute the addition)}
\\ &= y_i \paren{\logf{\red{\pi_i}} - \logf{\teal{1-\pi_i}}} + \logf{1-\pi_i}
&& \text{(factor out } y_i \text{)}
\\ &= y_i \logf{\frac{\red{\pi_i}}{\teal{1-\pi_i}}} + \logf{1-\pi_i}
&& \text{(difference of logs is the log of the quotient)}
\\ &= y_i \logit(\pi_i) + \logf{1-\pi_i}
&& \text{(log-odds identity: } \logit(\pi) = \logf{\pi/(1-\pi)} \text{)}
\\ &= y_i \eta_i + \logf{1-\pi_i}
&& \text{(definition: } \eta_i \eqdef \logit(\pi_i) \text{)}
\\ &= y_i \eta_i + \logf{\invf{1+\odds_i}}
&& \text{(non-event probability: } 1-\pi_i = \invf{1+\odds_i} \text{)}
\\ &= y_i \eta_i - \logf{1+\odds_i}
&& \text{(log of a reciprocal)}
\ea
$$

:::

:::{#lem-logistic-loglik-component}

#### Per-observation log-likelihood component

$$\ell_i(\pi_i) = y_i \eta_i - \logf{1+\odds_i}$$

:::
